preamble: The transformation of the voiceless uvular plosive /q/ to the voiced alveolar affricate /dz/ is a notable phonetic shift observed in various dialects of Najdi Arabic. This complex change involves a series of articulatory modifications, including fronting, voicing, and affrication, often triggered by specific vocalic environments.
phoneticEffects: This shift is characterized by a significant fronting of the place of articulation from uvular to alveolar, accompanied by a change in manner from a plosive to an affricate, and a change in voicing from voiceless to voiced
sources: Al-Qahtani, A. (2023). The Historical Changes of /k/ and /q/ in Najdi Arabic: A Phonological Analysis. Theory and Practice in Language Studies, 13(4), 797-802.
Al-Jarf, R. (2014). Sounds Phenomena in the Dialect of Skaka-Al Jouf: It's Relation to the Modern Standard Arabic. EA Journals, 2(1), 1-12.
